spl_product_data_elements: Head Lice Treatment Ivermectin Lotion118mL Ivermectin IVERMECTIN IVERMECTIN DIMETHICONE ALOE BARBADENSIS LEAF JUICE EDETATE DISODIUM GLYCERYL STEARATE CETYL ALCOHOL PHENOXYETHANOL ETHYLHEXYLGLYCERIN PEG-100 STEARATE TOCOPHEROL CETEARYL ALCOHOL CARBOMER PANTHENOL WATER CAPRYLIC/CAPRIC TRIGLYCERIDE GLYCERIN ALLANTOIN
do_not_use: Do not use on broken or severely irritated skin.
when_using: When using this product Avoid contact with eyes. If contact occurs, rinse thoroughly with water. Use only on the scalp and hair. keep eyes tightly closed and protect eyes with a washcloth or towel if product gets into eyes, gently flush with water eye redness, soreness, or irritation can occur dandruff, dry skin, or burning sensation of the skin can occur use only on scalp and hair
stop_use: Stop use and ask a doctor if Skin irritation, redness, or rash develops and persists. Signs of an allergic reaction occur. Head lice remain after treatment or symptoms worsen. breathing difficulty occurs eye irritation occurs skin or scalp irritation continues or infection occurs rash develops
keep_out_of_reach_of_children: If swallowed, get medical help or contact a Poison Control Center right away.
ask_doctor_or_pharmacist: have any skin conditions or sensitivities are pregnant or plan to become pregnant are currently or planning to breastfeed
dosage_and_administration: Children 6 months to under 12 years of age: Adult supervision is required. Adults and children 6 months of age and over: hair and scalp must be dry before applying product apply enough lotion to completely cover dry hair and scalp completely cover the scalp and hair closest to the scalp first, then apply outward toward the ends of the hair rub product throughout hair leave on hair and scalp for 10 minutes after hair and scalp are completely covered after 10 minutes, rinse product completely from hair and scalp using only water after rinsing, dry and style hair as usual wait 24 hours before applying shampoo nit combing is not necessary for treatment to work, but a fine-tooth comb may be used to remove dead lice and nits if desired do not use again on the same person and same lice infestation without talking to a healthcare provider first if infestation continues, see a doctor for other treatments
inactive_ingredient: Water Caprylic/Capric Triglyceride Glycerin Cetearyl Alcohol Glyceryl Stearate PEG-100 Stearate Cetyl Alcohol Dimethicone Panthenol Allantoin Aloe Barbadensis Leaf Juice Tocopherol Carbomer Disodium EDTA Phenoxyethanol Ethylhexylglycerin
